name: Research paper section (normal)
markdown:
# Hölder Inequalities

This introduction motivates the work in plain prose and sets up the notation used throughout, deliberately without any mathematics so the text layer would be clean.

## Methods

We define the setup carefully. The exponents satisfy a duality relation and the proof proceeds by a standard convexity argument applied term by term to each summand.

$$\sum_{i=1}^n |a_i b_i| \le \left(\sum_i |a_i|^p\right)^{1/p} \left(\sum_i |b_i|^q\right)^{1/q}$$

### Data

| model | score |
| --- | --- |
| A | 0.91 |
| B | 0.88 |

## Results

The results confirm the bound is tight. We discuss several consequences and edge cases that arise when one of the exponents tends to infinity, with detailed commentary throughout.
